record.text:
I was informed that my open-source maintainer free access to GitHub Copilot was revoked.

I’ve never been more excited to lose free access to something. It caused so many issues with my employer’s GitHub organization.

I miss the days of bug reports that are, “I do this and that happens, but I expect this other thing to happen. Here are the steps I follow to reproduce.”

Now everything is fed to AI and you get a root cause analysis that may or may not be a wild goose chase.

I recently discovered a new use for LLMs. It helped me create a personal color scheme based on my preferences and generate theme configurations for all the tools I use. A clean, pleasing, and consistent experience for my whole computer experience.

I just saw a chain of comments where people were arguing about something and one person went “it’s true, just CoPilot it”

I’m gonna walk into the river.
You can also be a morally good person and a bad character. Sometimes being too perfect is boring and uncompelling.

A character doesn’t not have to be morally good for them to be good as in well written/interesting!!! I’m begging you all to understand that. Lex Luthor is a good character but an evil diva. Otto Hightower is good character but an evil man. Please get that in your head.

Power Platform + Dynamics 365 ship with a maze of agreements. Here's what the official legal landing page actually means — the interesting, surprising, and genuinely impactful bits — in human language.

29 June 2026
⏱️ ~6 min read
☕ Read this first
This is a Copilot Cowork interpretation of Microsoft's Business Applications legal landing page, rewritten for humans. It is not legal advice and is not the contract. Where this summary and the official terms ever disagree, the official terms win. Always click through before you make a decision.

Start here
🗺️
The legal landing page is a map, not the contract
Microsoft is explicit that this page is just a convenience overview of the terms covering Power Platform and Dynamics 365 (together, "Business Applications"). The documents it points to are the things you actually agreed to — and it warns that other services, software, and content "may be governed by additional or different terms not listed." Translation: the page is a directory. The binding rules live elsewhere, and the list isn't even exhaustive.

record.text: This builds on what Amanda Natividad @amandanat at SparkToro and Koen Stam at GTMcraft keep circling from different angles: the durable edge is human, specific, and dies when left undefined.

If you cannot write your 20% down in 15 minutes, you do not have a leverage problem.

You have a clarity problem.

Name your 20. Protect it on the calendar. Let the machine have the rest.

The loop:

Run the 80 on a schedule (Microsoft Copilot).
Capture the 20 as it happens (Notion).
Push recurring jobs to a scheduled layer (Claude CoWork).

The 80 gets cheaper. The 20 gets sharper. The gap compounds.

external.description:
The shift from AI-assisted to AI-native is redefining who builds the best digital products. Here are the companies that have genuinely restructured their delivery model around AI.

💡

****TL;DR:****
- In 2026, AI-native product development means AI entering the process at the strategy and design layer, not just at the coding stage.
- This guide covers six companies evaluated on consistent criteria, including production AI track record, EU AI Act readiness, and delivery model transparency.
- All companies on this list are mid-size boutique firms with senior access and fast decision-making. No large generalist agencies.

**What Makes a Company AI-Native in 2026?**

The term gets overused, but the distinction is real. An AI-native product company doesn't just use Copilot to write faster code or drop a ChatGPT API into the backend. It applies AI at the product decision layer: during discovery, scoping, prototyping, and validation, not only at the implementation layer.

The clearest signal is not which AI tools a company uses. It's how early in the product process those tools start influencing decisions.

**Review Criteria**

Each company was assessed using the following criteria, applied consistently across all entries.

  * AI integration depth – whether AI is applied at product strategy and design, or only at the coding layer
  * Production track record – AI systems shipped and running at scale, not proof-of-concept demos
  * EU AI Act readiness – understanding of risk classification, compliance, and human oversight obligations
  * Delivery model – team structure, engagement model, and decision-making speed
  * Client portfolio – industries served, recognizable clients, and project complexity
  * Independent reviews – ratings from Clutch and other third-party platforms



**6 Best AI-Native Product Development Companies in 2026**

**Company** |  **Founded** |  **HQ** |  **AI Integration Layer** |  **Typical Projects** |  **Notable Clients**
---|---|---|---|---|---
Boldare |  2004 |  Gliwice, Poland |  Strategy, design & engineering |  Full product lifecycle, MCP servers, AI agents |  BlaBlaCar, Bosch, Decathlon, UNDP
Notch |  ~2021 |  Central Europe |  Engineering |  Legacy modernization, agentic development |  Enterprise clients
DBB Software |  N/A |  Europe |  Engineering |  AI-augmented workflows, regulated industries |  Security-conscious clients
Tooploox |  2013 |  Poland |  Engineering + ML Research |  Computer vision, NLP, production ML systems |  Various product companies
Synoviq |  N/A |  Europe / Remote |  Strategy + Engineering |  Fast-start AI product development |  Scale-up clients
Leobit |  2014 |  Ukraine / Europe |  Engineering |  Team augmentation, .NET + AI integration |  Mid-market scale-ups

**1. Boldare**

HQ: Gliwice, Poland | Founded: 2004 | Team: 250+ | Clutch: 4.9

Boldare is one of Europe's most established AI-native product design and development companies, with over 20 years of experience and 250+ products shipped across fintech, energy, logistics, medtech, and consumer tech. Clients include BlaBlaCar, Bosch, Sonnen, Decathlon, and UNDP.

**Key Features:**

  * AI product development and consulting from strategy through launch, including MCP server development, agentic AI implementation, LLM integration, and API development
  * Vibe coding sprints and legacy code modernization with AI, plus AI-powered QA, workflow automation, and sales AI enablement
  * AI adoption consulting for engineering organizations navigating the shift to AI-native workflows



**Why choose them:** The right partner for founders who want a single company to own the entire product journey with AI embedded at every stage. Boldare is the benchmark for AI-native product delivery in Europe.

**2. Notch**

HQ: Central Europe | Specialization: Modernization and agentic development

Notch is a newer AI-native engineering company focused on applied AI, agentic development, and enterprise modernization. It works primarily with organizations that need to restructure existing systems around AI without the risk and cost of a full rewrite.

**Key Features:**

  * Agentic development and applied AI for enterprise systems
  * Legacy architecture modernization with AI capabilities layered in incrementally
  * Boutique structure with direct senior engineer access from day one



**Why choose them:** A strong fit for product teams dealing with legacy infrastructure who want to move toward AI-native delivery without starting from scratch.

**3. DBB Software**

HQ: Europe | Specialization: Regulated industries

DBB Software has carved out a niche in AI-assisted development for security-conscious and compliance-heavy clients. Their practice covers AI-augmented engineering with a strong focus on data governance, audit trails, and risk classification.

**Key Features:**

  * AI-augmented engineering workflows for regulated environments
  * Strong focus on data governance, audit trails, and risk classification
  * Boutique structure with faster decisions and senior access throughout the engagement



**Why choose them:** Suitable for companies in healthcare, finance, or legal that need AI development with compliance built in from the start, not bolted on at the end.

**4. Tooploox**

HQ: Poland | Founded: 2013 | Specialization: ML systems

Tooploox combines software engineering with a research-grade machine learning practice. It is one of the more technically credible options in Poland for companies building AI-first products, with a team that bridges academic-level AI research and commercial product delivery.

**Key Features:**

  * Computer vision and NLP development at production quality
  * Research-grade ML practice embedded directly in delivery teams
  * Track record of shipping ML systems that hold up under real user behavior, not only in demos



**Why choose them:** The right choice for companies where the AI system itself is the core product, not a feature layer added on top of standard software.

**5. Synoviq**

HQ: Europe / Remote | Engagement model: Fast-start intensive

Synoviq is built for product teams that have a clear direction but need external engineering capacity to move quickly. Its fast-engagement model cuts the multi-week onboarding typical of larger agencies.

**Key Features:**

  * Three-day strategy intensive followed by a weekly release cadence
  * AI and data engineering with a fast, predictable delivery model
  * Remote-first with flexible team composition



**Why choose them:** A good option for scale-ups that need to move fast and want structured delivery from the first week.

**How to Evaluate an AI-Native Development Partner**

Before signing anything, a few questions that actually separate genuine AI-native companies from agencies that updated their homepage in 2023.

**Ask about AI systems in production, not prototypes.** Any competent team can demo a proof of concept. Ask for specifics: which model, which infrastructure, what failure modes they hit, and how they resolved them.

**Find out where AI enters the process.** If the answer is "we use Copilot for coding," that is an AI-assisted workflow, not an AI-native one. Look for AI being used at product scoping, user research synthesis, or architecture decisions.

**Assess EU AI Act readiness.** For any product touching employment, credit, healthcare, or education, your partner needs to understand risk classification and human oversight obligations. Ask directly.

**Look for intellectual honesty.** Partners who publish what they have learned, including failures, are the ones genuinely operating at the frontier.

**FAQs**

**What is an AI-native product development company?**

One that applies AI at the product decision layer: during discovery, scoping, and prototyping, not only at the implementation stage.

**What is the EU AI Act and why does it matter?**

The EU AI Act is in force in 2026. For high-risk domains like employment, credit, or healthcare, it introduces requirements around risk classification, data governance, and human oversight. Your development partner should understand these obligations from the start.

**What is MCP server development?**

MCP (Model Context Protocol) is the standard for connecting AI agents to business systems such as CRMs, databases, and internal APIs. It is foundational infrastructure for any serious AI agent deployment.

**What is the best AI-native product development company in Europe?**

The right partner depends on your product stage, technical needs, and delivery model preference.

record.text: U.S. export controls handed Huawei China's AI chip market, and Nvidia's own CEO now says the company is effectively foreclosed from competing there.

external.description:
Nvidia once had 95% market share of AI chips in China. But that market share is close to zero now.

Following U.S. export control measures imposed against Nvidia, starting in 2022, China compelled its technology firms to purchase domestically produced chips. Huawei stepped into the void created by Nvidia and has been steadily expanding ever since.

According to an analysis from Bernstein Research, Nvidia's market share of China's AI chip market will drop to around 8% in 2026, as that of Huawei grows to 50%. Jensen Huang, the CEO of Nvidia, has admitted the company is no longer competitive in the Chinese data center market.

This is one of the quickest market share declines in semiconductors and it took less than five years to occur.

## How Nvidia Lost Its Grip on China

NVIDIA's CEO Jensen Huang remains one of the most recognisable figures in the AI industry. During a recent visit to Beijing, he attracted crowds while eating traditional zhajiangmian noodles, highlighting the company’s popularity among Chinese developers and businesses.

external.description:
Sony and Arc System Works have officially unveiled the final character team coming to Marvel Tōkon: Fighting Souls, introducing Blade, Loki, and Deadpool alongside the previously announced Ghost Rider as the game's fifth and final squad, the Samurai Outriders.

The reveal closed out EVO 2026 on June 28 with a brand-new trailer, giving fans their best look yet at three of Marvel's most unpredictable antiheroes in action while also confirming a new stage, fresh gameplay mechanics, and the game's first global Open Beta.

With launch drawing closer, the announcement offers the clearest picture yet of what Marvel Tōkon's final roster will look like, and it's arguably the game's most unconventional team.

Here are the highlights of our new AI Policy:
Tidal will identify and tag AI-generated music in our app. Listeners will see an "AI" badge next to music we detect as wholly AI-generated.
Tidal will not tolerate AI-generated music that impersonates an artist or group, or that facilitates fraudulent activity. We're implementing automatic tools to remove these releases immediately and on an ongoing basis.
Tidal will not allow music that is 100% AI-generated to be monetized. No royalties will go to such releases, nor will AI-generated uploads be eligible for direct-to-fan sales.
We will expand these policies to music that is substantially AI-generated when AI detection technology is sufficiently reliable to do so.
You'll start seeing these changes from July 15.

record.text:
Great work from Tidal

- All AI music labeled
- Imitations of bands taken down 
- No royalties for 100% AI generated music 
- A promise to extend this to partially AI generated music when they’ve got the tech to do it. 

You LOVE to see it 👏👏👏

Full text in alt

images.alt:
POLICY UPDATE
Our stance on AI-generated music
Hi,
AI music generation tools are changing how music is created and distributed. As this technology evolves, Tidal is introducing platform standards to protect artists, their craft, and inform listeners.
Here are the highlights of our new AI Policy:
Tidal will identify and tag AI-generated music in our app. Listeners will see an "AI" badge next to music we detect as wholly AI-generated.
Tidal will not tolerate AI-generated music that impersonates an artist or group, or that facilitates fraudulent activity. We're implementing automatic tools to remove these releases immediately and on an ongoing basis.
Tidal will not allow music that is 100% AI-generated to be monetized. No royalties will go to such releases, nor will AI-generated uploads be eligible for direct-to-fan sales.
We will expand these policies to music that is substantially AI-generated when AI detection technology is sufficiently reliable to do so.
You'll start seeing these changes from July 15.
Check out the full policy here. To learn more, please visit our FAQ.
For the music,
The Tidal Team
